title: Scorecard
description:
Top-level scorecard artifact.

Serializes as `scorecard.json` and is the trust boundary for the renderer. The committed JSON Schema at `.config/scorecard/schema.json` (regenerated by the `emit-schema` binary) is the validator's contract.

all_check_runs_url.description: External link to the full Check Runs page on the head commit, so the comment can satisfy "every failing gate is one click away" even when more than three gates fail.

fallback_thresholds_active.description:
`true` when the producer resolved row statuses using the hardcoded [`threshold::ThresholdConfig::fallback`] thresholds because the operator `quality.toml` was absent or empty. A present-but-malformed or unreadable `quality.toml` is a loud failure (non-zero exit, no artifact written), not a silent fallback — so this flag is `true` only for the deliberate "operator hasn't tuned thresholds yet" case.

The renderer keys off this flag to emit [`threshold::STARTER_PREAMBLE`] + [`threshold::FALLBACK_MARKER`] + [`threshold::PATH_HINT_COMMENT`] so operators can tell at a glance that the verdict came from starter-wheel defaults rather than their tuned thresholds.

overall_status.description: Resolved status across all rows. The renderer's banner reads this directly; per the .feature spec the banner status is the worst status across all rows.

pr.description: Pull request metadata captured at producer time so the renderer can render without a separate `pr-meta.json` lookup.

schema_version.description: Schema version. Future row variants bump per the migration playbook in `decisions/mokumo/adr-scorecard-crate-shape.md`.

top_failures.description: Top-failing gate Check Run links (max three per the two-click rule). May be omitted on the wire; deserialization fills `vec![]`. Producers SHOULD emit explicitly so the wire contract is unambiguous.

BddFeatureBreakout.description: One crate's *feature-file* drill-down for the `BddFeatureLevelSkipped` row. Counts whole `.feature` files as the unit — a feature with `@wip` at the feature line counts as one skipped feature regardless of how many scenarios it contains. The `by_tag` counts surface which tag families the operator is using (e.g. `@wip` vs `@future` vs `@tracked:#1234`).

by_tag.description: Per-tag drill-down across the crate's *feature-level* tags. Counts increment once per file (not once per scenario).

feature_skipped.description: `.feature` files carrying at least one feature-level skip tag.

BddScenarioBreakout.description: One crate's *scenario* drill-down for the `BddScenarioLevelSkipped` row. Counts individual scenarios whose skip tag lives on the scenario itself (not inherited from a feature-level tag — those land in `BddFeatureBreakout`).

by_tag.description: Per-tag drill-down across the crate's *scenario-level* skip tags. Counts increment once per scenario.

scenario_skipped.description: Scenarios whose own tag set carries a skip tag — does NOT count scenarios whose only skip tag was inherited from the feature.

scenario_total.description: Total scenarios across the crate's `.feature` files.

Breakouts.description: Coverage drill-down breakouts shown beneath the [`Row::CoverageDelta`] summary cell. V4 ships the type with `by_crate` populated by the coverage producer; the per-handler `handlers` vec stays empty until the per-handler-branch tooling (mokumo#583) ships.

CoverageAxis.description:
One handler's negative-path coverage axis for the `HandlerCoverageAxis` row (Quinn blind-spot 3).

Boolean columns mark whether a scenario for that axis exists in BDD — a `false` is the actionable signal (negative path uncovered).

handlers.description: Per-handler branch coverage entries. Empty until the producer (mokumo#583) ships; the renderer surfaces a `(per-handler producer pending — see #583)` note inline when the vec is empty.

line_delta_pp.description: Per-crate line coverage delta in percentage points vs base.

GateRun.description: Reference to a single failing gate's Check Run, surfaced in `top_failures` for the two-click rule.

url.description: Absolute https:// URL to the Check Run page.

HandlerBreakout.description: Per-handler branch coverage entry for the [`CoverageDelta::breakouts`] drill-down (Quinn blind-spot 3 sibling — surfaces uncovered handler negative paths inside the coverage row).

MutationSurvivor.description:
One mutation that survived for the `MutationSurvivors` row's top-N.

V4 ships the variant + supporting type. The producer (`cargo-mutants --in-diff`, mokumo#748) populates instances in a follow-up PR once the upstream gate ships; until then `top_survivors` is stub-empty.

file.description: Source file the surviving mutant lives in.

PrMeta.description: Pull request metadata — the renderer's identity for the sticky comment.

is_fork.description: Whether the PR was opened from a fork. Renderer uses this to scope Check Run links against the fork's head commit per the .feature spec.

Row.description:
Tagged-union of scorecard row variants.

`#[serde(tag = "type")]` lands in the JSON Schema as a `oneOf` with per-variant `type` constants, which the Layer 2 post-processor walks to inject the `failure_detail_md` invariant.

`#[non_exhaustive]` on both the enum and each variant blocks external crates from struct-literal construction (variant-level) and from non-wildcard pattern matches (enum-level). Within this crate, both remain reachable so the producer binary can construct rows and the renderer-side dest types can match exhaustively.

Every variant carries a `failure_detail_md: Option<String>` so the generic Layer 2 post-processor in `schema_postprocess.rs` can inject the `if status == Red then failure_detail_md required` invariant uniformly across the `oneOf`.

anchor.description: Anchor fragment for jump-linking from the comment.

breakouts.description: Per-crate (and eventually per-handler-branch) drill-down. V4 emits an empty default when no per-crate signal is wired upstream; the renderer surfaces a `(per-handler producer pending — see #583)` note inline when `by_crate[].handlers` is empty.

delta_pp.description: Raw coverage delta in percentage points vs. the base branch. The constructor records this alongside the status that was minted from it; the renderer keeps using `delta_text` for display, but downstream tooling (and future trend dashboards) can read `delta_pp` without reparsing the formatted string.

delta_text.description: Display string for the delta (e.g. `"+0.3 pp"` or `"-4.2 pp"`).

failure_detail_md.description: Inline failure detail rendered below the row when status is Red. JSON Schema requires this when `status == "Red"`; the Red constructor enforces the same invariant at compile time for external callers.

id.description: Stable row id (e.g. `"coverage"`). Used by the renderer for anchors.

status.description: Resolved status (Green/Yellow/Red). Set by the constructor — callers cannot supply this directly because `Row::CoverageDelta` is `#[non_exhaustive]`.

oneOf.description: CRAP delta — count of functions over the configured CRAP threshold, minus the same count on the base branch. Producer is `crap4rs` / `crap4ts` (the `--format scorecard-row` projector). See the gap- analysis pipeline note for the V3-symmetric shape rationale.

anchor.description: Anchor fragment for jump-linking from the comment.

delta_count.description: Signed delta vs. baseline (positive = new violations landed). Mirrors `CoverageDelta::delta_pp`'s sign discipline.

id.description: Stable row id (e.g. `"coverage"`). Used by the renderer for anchors.

threshold.description: CRAP threshold in effect on this run (default 15). Metric- intrinsic; surfaced so downstream tooling can interpret `delta_count` without out-of-band knowledge.

oneOf.description: Top-N mutations not killed by the test suite for this PR. Producer is `cargo-mutants --in-diff` (mokumo#748). V4 ships the variant; row-population is a follow-up PR when the producer lands.

anchor.description: Anchor fragment for jump-linking from the comment.

id.description: Stable row id (e.g. `"coverage"`). Used by the renderer for anchors.

survivor_count.description: Total surviving mutants for the diff (top-N is the renderer's drill-down; this is the headline count).

top_survivors.description: Up to three top survivors selected by the producer.

oneOf.description: Count of `.feature` files that carry a *feature-level* skip tag (`@wip`, `@future`, `@tracked:*`). Feature-level tags propagate to every scenario in the file, so this row tracks *backlog* — how many work-streams are still gated open. The scenario-level signal lives in `BddScenarioLevelSkipped`.

anchor.description: Anchor fragment for jump-linking from the comment.

by_tag.description: Workspace-wide tag breakdown across feature-level tags (`{ "@wip": 10, "@future": 2 }`).

id.description: Stable row id (e.g. `"coverage"`). Used by the renderer for anchors.

skipped_features.description: Feature files carrying at least one feature-level skip tag.

total_features.description: Total `.feature` files across the discovered roots.

oneOf.description: Count of *individual scenarios* whose own tag set carries a skip tag — feature-level inheritance is excluded so the count tracks targeted hygiene debt.

anchor.description: Anchor fragment for jump-linking from the comment.

by_tag.description: Workspace-wide tag breakdown across scenario-level skip tags (`{ "@wip": 8, "@future": 5, "@tracked:#1234": 2 }`).

id.description: Stable row id (e.g. `"coverage"`). Used by the renderer for anchors.

skipped_scenarios.description: Scenarios whose own tag set carries a skip tag.

oneOf.description: Per-gate Check Run summary (Layer 1 of the 3-layer DAG). V4 ships the variant + ctors; V5 (#770) populates `gate_runs` with per-gate Check Run references and wires the two-click rule.

anchor.description: Anchor fragment for jump-linking from the comment.

gate_runs.description: Per-gate Check Run references. V4 stub uses an empty vec pinned to mokumo#770; V5 populates with real runs.

id.description: Stable row id (e.g. `"coverage"`). Used by the renderer for anchors.

oneOf.description: Flaky-test population (Quinn blind-spot 1). Producer scans for `// FLAKY:` source markers and consumes nextest retry events on the same SHA.

anchor.description: Anchor fragment for jump-linking from the comment.

flaky_marker_count.description: Count of `// FLAKY:` markers in the source tree.

id.description: Stable row id (e.g. `"coverage"`). Used by the renderer for anchors.

nextest_retry_events.description: Count of nextest retry events on the head SHA (best-effort — `0` when no retry-event JSON is supplied).

oneOf.description: Cumulative test wall-clock delta vs base (Quinn blind-spot 2). Producer aggregates per-job durations from the GitHub Actions API and compares against the base branch's most recent run.

anchor.description: Anchor fragment for jump-linking from the comment.

delta_seconds.description: Signed delta vs. base branch's most recent main run. Negative means CI got faster.

id.description: Stable row id (e.g. `"coverage"`). Used by the renderer for anchors.

total_ci_seconds.description: Cumulative CI wall-clock for this run (sum of job durations, in seconds).

oneOf.description: Per-handler negative-path coverage axes (Quinn blind-spot 3). Producer reads the BDD scenario × handler map (mokumo#654 / #655) and emits one [`CoverageAxis`] per handler.

anchor.description: Anchor fragment for jump-linking from the comment.

id.description: Stable row id (e.g. `"coverage"`). Used by the renderer for anchors.

oneOf.description:
Embedded changed-scope Mermaid diagram showing the diff's dependency footprint (frontend graph + crate graph). Producer consumes `dependency-cruiser` JSON (mokumo#749 / PR #774) plus `cargo metadata` and renders a `graph LR` snippet.

Truncates above the 200-node Mermaid renderer limit; oversize diagrams emit a `(diagram truncated — see workflow logs)` footer in `mermaid_md`.

anchor.description: Anchor fragment for jump-linking from the comment.

id.description: Stable row id (e.g. `"coverage"`). Used by the renderer for anchors.

mermaid_md.description: Mermaid markdown (already wrapped in a fenced code block).

node_count.description: Total node count in the (possibly truncated) diagram.

Status.description:
Status icon shown on the banner and per-row.

String-tagged so JSON Schema post-processors can match against `{ "const": "Red" }` to require `failure_detail_md` on Red rows.

count.description: Number of scenarios bearing this tag in the crate.

tag.description: Tag literal as it appears in the .feature file (`@wip`, `tracked:mokumo#123`, ...).
